item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY SA 3.0) due to being a still from the TV series Fringe, which has not entered the public domain. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was between downvote and novote, but it will be downvote in the end.

This one isn't old enough to have merit on the trope of the leaking universe alone. It isn't that good a rendition of the trope, either. It isn't hard to compare to Alexylva, and it isn't nearly as good as their very first skip.

Nothing against recycling ideas, but I do expect a rewriting someday that gets more from the potential of this story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late to the party on this one but this article didn't really do anything for me. The airships don't really do anything and Rupertia doesn't seem particularly unique or interesting.

Downvote from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The geopolitics of the alternate reality nations is interesting indeed, but the trope of alternate universe zeppelins and outcome of the narrative render the overall article lacking. The propagation of state propaganda (in this manner) can be likened to 1984, except that 1984 pulled it off to a greater effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Mobile Task Force 59 Phi-Sigma ("Hindenburg Haters")" This received the reaction of the unusual genuine LOL, and thus a +1 before finishing the article. that said this SCP brought a to my mind the mysterious airships of the late 1800's, a SCP with real world ancestry is one I appreciate when not done by overreaching.

http://www.keelynet.com/gravity/aero1.htm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Original image by panina.anna can be found here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rupertia is indeed a bit too generic (though not extremely so, at least there was effort put in :P), but overall I liked the article enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Count me as another one insufficiently engaged by Rupertia and the general nature of the parallel reality to feel this was worthy of an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.

A lot of people are saying Rupertia is generic. Maybe I'll write a tale about them. I've got a basic idea of how their society is run, so perhaps I can satisfy the curiosity of a few folks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rupertia is indeed utterly generic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, in that case, if they have this capability, why wouldn't the messages themselves just be teleported/manifested/whatever to the cities instead of the automated Zeppelin that drop them? Also, I personally would have liked to see something that reflected more coherence about the article as a whole. For example, have the Zeppelins being tested in their transportation abilities for use in the war in Rupertia. Or something. I dunno, it might just be me. Also, I kinda wish Rupertia itself was more interesting and distinguishing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're automated, so there's no crew to point out they're not in the right place. The demanifestation could be a defense mechanism.

I admit, my thought surrounding this was "how can I get Zeppelins to pop up out of nowhere?", because I love Zeps. I added in Rupertia to make it more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um… Why exactly are these things here…? I mean, they apparently realize that they're manifesting and demanifesting, given that they do so when approached, but why our dimension and how do they not realize it's the correct world? I feel like you just shoehorned in a way to make this into an article by having random aircraft just manifest and drop details about some other dimensional war that holds some sort of storyline. Furthermore on that point, the war itself wasn't that interesting, and wasn't really distinguishable from a lot of wars over time. So, downvoted.

item.title: Re: my first post :D
item.content:encoded: I will try i'm writing my first scp as we speak, it is going to be about a creature that is directly tied with the negative being in every religion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The zeppelins really aren't interesting enough on their own (the moment I saw the first line of the description, I knew they would be both from another dimension and more technologically advanced than any we created) and the article seems too focused on Rupertia, which is just kind of your standard crazy empire in another dimension. I really can't get worked up enough to downvote, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted. Rupertia really isn't inspiring any interest for me, and comes across as really generic. Since the entire article hinges off of Rupertia…yeah. It's not working.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I was going to neutralvote this, but then I saw the MTF name. It's just so amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Thanks! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mobile Task Force 59 Phi-Sigma ("Hindenberg Haters")

Just a note that the famous crashed German airship is normally spelt "Hindenburg".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm, so are the dimensional shifts of the zeppelins a result of a technology malfunction, or is it a result of weapon used in the war having completely screwed up the fabric of spacetime?

Foundation agents shall monitor news networks in the United States and Canada for any reports of unusual aircraft activity.

Nitpick: shouldn't there be something about Foundation agents embedded in air traffic control?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "what if a bunch of zeppelins started showing up in our world inexplicably?" is the thought preoccupying my mind 90% of the time anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dangit. I knew someone would figure out where I got that picture from. :P

Really, though, the zeppelins came from Fringe, but everything else just spawned from my own imagination. I took the idea of "what if a bunch of zeppelins started showing up in our world inexplicably?" and went from there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I had to guess, there's quite a bit of Chinese influence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I enjoy watching Fringe too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you look closely, I put in a few subtle hints:

The Respected Stephen XI

An unusual title for a monarch, don't you think?

…it is the will of the Cosmos…

Notice "Cosmos" being capitalized, and deliberate omission of any references to a higher power.

There's more here than meets the eye. Hint: they're not atheists. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Nothing to see here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rupertia is at war with Alaria. Rupertia has always been at war with Eastasia Alaria. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm debating about this. The information delivery system is pretty dang cool, but it's not related to the scare in the end, and the flyers' story is just too generic. I saw where it was going at the first, but I enjoyed reading it. Hm. No vote either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two of the flyers end with "Long love the Emperor", but one ends with "Long live the Emperor".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the dirigibles and I don't dislike Rupertia. But the combination doesn't work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But have they always been at war with Alaria?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could almost HEAR the information ministry face-palming about the content of the 2nd message in their 3rd message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck yeah zeppelins.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It also sounds incredibly antiquated to me. I'm siding with the Rupertian ShallNots.

item.title: Re: Perhaps.
item.content:encoded:
Damn, that was my second guess. I thought it was a reference to the plant.

Alaria is a kind of plant, too, so I thought it was some kind of extended plant in-joke, instead of a history reference, which is what I usually think everything is anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, although I had a moment of mild ambiguity in the sentence indicating that when an aircraft approach the zeppelin, "it" disappears. After a moment, I realized it was the zeppelin disappearing, but I had a moment when I imagined the it was the aircraft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Shall" connotes command and and necessity. I think it's an excellent thing to have show up in SCP containment procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'Shall' sounds right to me, but maybe thats because as an engineer most requirements we are exposed to are written using X shall Y (paramater Z).

edit: I should also probably clarify that I always think of containment procedures as a set of requirements for the storage of the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I will reveal that one of the national pastimes is jokingly misspelling its name. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. Rupertia needs to be more interesting.

item.title: Re: my first post :D
item.content:encoded: Feel free to write a tale about Rupertia, if you'd like. I just made it up for this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Yes it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is canon now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pictured: Rupertia's Great Emperor

item.title: Re: Perhaps.
item.content:encoded:
Rupertia*.

Oh gods, now the misspelling of Rupertia is going to become an in-joke…>.>

item.title: Re: Perhaps.
item.content:encoded: What do I win? Can I be Emperor of Rupertaria for a day!? Pleeeease?

item.title: Perhaps.
item.content:encoded:
But I never said it was an alternate reality. I merely said it was where I got the title. For all we know, Rupertia doesn't exist and the zeppelins are just [GOI of choice] fucking with us. ;)

But yes, British Rupert's Land was the origin of the name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since these things manifested over, well, North America, and they refer to their world as 'Earth', I'll hazard a guess:

Rupert's Land breaks away and forms its own monarchy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you interested in botany at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed all instances of "King" to "Emperor". D'oh!

Also, bonus points to whoever can figure out where I got the name from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice.

Minor nitpick: Shouldn't it be "Long live the Emperor!" and "The Emperor is dead"?

+1 regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I use it to sound fanchy-schmancy when I'm writing. I've seen it used a handful of other times, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An I the only one that's bugged from the word "shall" being used the containment procedures? It might just be me, but it sounds strange.

item.title: Re: my first post :D
item.content:encoded:
Like I said above, I think it'd be better to keep Rupertia's origins ambiguous. That may just be me, though.
At any rate, I wouldn't mind seeing Rupertia turn up again in the future.

Welcome to the site, BTW. :D

item.title: my first post :D
item.content:encoded:
It sounds like the manifestations are from a different world that is leaking into our own. You could possibly add a incident were one of the manifestations were in the middle of a battle and is destroyed but leaves nothing behind, but a note from a passenger.
+1 I enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually kind of like the juxtaposition between Rupertia's generic political scenario and the bizarrely advanced dirigibles that don't appear to have been sent here intentionally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess I must've missed something, because this is the first I've heard of Rupertia and the site search engine only linked me back to this skip.

I really had to think about this one before voting. On the one hand, I thought the idea of an anomalous continent unknown to the rest of the world sounded pretty cool. On the other hand, it seemed more likely to me that the author intended for this to be some sort of "leakage" from an alternate universe/timeline, which isn't as interesting. In the end I decided the ambiguity probably works better than actually knowing, so I upboated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally find it a bit too shallow. Rupertania seems pretty generic from what little we see of it. Just a standard totalitarian empire fighting a losing war.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm interested by the glimpses we get into this Rupertania country. While I think the object could have had a bit more detail to it that could tell us more about the country, the flyers do their job well. The last entry wraps things up nicely.
